clinical_study.brief_title: Nivolumab Plus Dabrafenib/Trametinib in BRAF V600E+ Stage IV Melanoma Progressing on Anti-PD-1 (COMBO-MEL)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Nivolumab Combined With Dabrafenib and Trametinib Versus Dabrafenib and Trametinib Alone in Patients With BRAF V600E-Mutant Stage IV Cutaneous Melanoma Who Have Progressed or Have Mixed Response on Anti-PD-1 Monotherapy

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates the combination of nivolumab (anti-PD-1) with dabrafenib plus trametinib (BRAF/MEK inhibitor doublet) versus dabrafenib/trametinib alone in patients with BRAF V600E-mutant Stage IV cutaneous melanoma who have evidence of progression or mixed/heterogeneous response (new lesions in one site with response in another) while receiving anti-PD-1 monotherapy. The hypothesis is that BRAF/MEK inhibition can overcome sites of resistance while synergizing with the pre-existing anti-tumor immune response.

detailed_description.textblock: Approximately 40-50% of cutaneous melanomas harbor BRAF V600E mutations, making them susceptible to BRAF/MEK inhibitor doublets (dabrafenib + trametinib). Anti-PD-1 therapy (nivolumab, pembrolizumab) and BRAF/MEK inhibition represent the two most active treatment strategies in metastatic melanoma. Mixed response on anti-PD-1 — defined as regression in some lesions concurrent with new or growing lesions elsewhere — occurs in approximately 10-15% of patients and represents a clinically challenging scenario. Preclinical data suggest that BRAF inhibition can enhance tumor antigen presentation and augment immune responses. This study randomizes patients with BRAF V600E-mutant Stage IV melanoma and mixed or confirmed progressive disease on anti-PD-1 monotherapy to nivolumab 240 mg Q2W + dabrafenib 150 mg BID + trametinib 2 mg/day versus dabrafenib 150 mg BID + trametinib 2 mg/day alone. Crossover is permitted at progression on the control arm. Stratification: presence of active brain metastases (yes vs no), LDH level (normal vs elevated), and prior ipilimumab (yes vs no).

primary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until radiographic progression or death, assessed up to 24 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Intracranial Response Rate (iRR) in patients with baseline brain metastases

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until death from any cause, up to 36 months
From first response to progression or death, up to 36 months
During treatment period, up to 24 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed Stage IV cutaneous melanoma (AJCC 8th edition M1a-d) - BRAF V600E mutation confirmed by a validated assay (e.g., cobas 4800 BRAF V600 Mutation Test, next-generation sequencing, or equivalent) - Mixed response or confirmed progression on most recent line of anti-PD-1 monotherapy (nivolumab or pembrolizumab) defined as: (a) RECIST 1.1 progressive disease in at least one target lesion, or (b) Appearance of one or more new lesions, with concurrent response or stable disease in other target lesions (mixed response/heterogeneous response) - Anti-PD-1 therapy must have been the most recent systemic therapy; patients may not have received prior BRAF or MEK inhibitors - At least one measurable lesion by RECIST 1.1 - Brain metastases are allowed if: neurologically stable, not requiring corticosteroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent, and either previously treated (SRS or surgery) or deemed suitable for systemic therapy by the treating physician - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 - Tumor mutational burden (TMB) and PD-L1 status collected at baseline (biomarker correlative; not required for eligibility) - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1000/mm3, platelets >= 75,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 8.5 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN (or <= 5x ULN with liver metastases) - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 40 mL/min - Adequate cardiac function: LVEF >= 50%; QTcF <= 480 ms - Women of childbearing potential must use adequate contraception; men must use contraception during treatment and for 6 months after last dose - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Non-cutaneous melanoma subtypes (uveal, mucosal, acral lentiginous) — these are excluded due to distinct biology - BRAF V600K, V600D, or other non-V600E BRAF mutations (may be enrolled in separate cohort at sponsor discretion) - Prior BRAF inhibitor (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) or MEK inhibitor (trametinib, cobimetinib, binimetinib) therapy - Prior ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) within 6 weeks of enrollment (washout allowed if > 6 weeks) - Confirmed complete progression on anti-PD-1 with ALL target lesions progressing; patients must have at least some evidence of residual response (mixed response eligibility) - Untreated symptomatic brain metastases requiring immediate neurosurgical intervention or corticosteroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent for symptom control - Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy (other than topical agents) - History of severe immune-mediated adverse events on prior anti-PD-1 (Grade 3-4 pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, or other irAE) - Active hepatitis B or C infection - Uncontrolled hypertension (> 150/100 mmHg) or history of hypertensive crisis - History of retinal vein occlusion or central serous retinopathy - Known hypersensitivity to nivolumab, dabrafenib, or trametinib - Pregnancy or breastfeeding - Concurrent participation in another interventional trial

intervention.description: Nivolumab 240 mg IV every 2 weeks (or 480 mg IV every 4 weeks per treating physician), plus dabrafenib 150 mg orally twice daily continuously, plus trametinib 2 mg orally once daily continuously, until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient withdrawal.

Dabrafenib 150 mg orally twice daily continuously, plus trametinib 2 mg orally once daily continuously, until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient withdrawal. Crossover to combination arm permitted at progression.
